claim: Infection of human T-cell lymphotropic virus type 1 is most frequent in individuals of African origin.

result.claim: Infection of human T-cell lymphotropic virus type 1 is most frequent in individuals of African origin.
result.reasoning: The available evidence confirms that HTLV-I infection is endemic in Africa, as stated in proposition prop_60 (Paper ID: 24512064). This proposition also lists Japan, the Caribbean, and Melanesia as other endemic regions. While Africa is clearly a region where HTLV-I is prevalent, the current evidence does not provide a comparative analysis of prevalence rates across these endemic regions to definitively state that the infection is *most frequent* in individuals of African origin. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence to fully support the claim's assertion of the highest frequency in this specific population.

evidence.text: Human T cell leukaemia viruses type I (HTLV-I) and type II (HTLV-II) are two exogenous retroviruses that infect humans.
evidence.source: HTLV-I/II associated disease in England and Wales, 1993-7: retrospective review of serology requests.

evidence.text: Delayed T cell recovery and restricted T cell receptor diversity after allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation are associated with increased risks of infection and cancer relapse.
evidence.source: Quantitative assessment of T-cell repertoire recovery after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation

evidence.text: The 95% confidence interval for the prevalence in those born in 1941-60 was 269 to 1596 per million.
evidence.source: Prevalent abnormal prion protein in human appendixes after bovine spongiform encephalopathy epizootic: large scale survey

evidence.text: West Nile virus (WNV) is the most common arthropod-borne flavivirus in the United States.
evidence.source: A C-Type Lectin Collaborates with a CD45 Phosphatase Homolog to Facilitate West Nile Virus Infection of Mosquitoes

evidence.text: HTLV-I infection is associated with a 0.25% lifetime risk of HTLV-I associated myelopathy.

evidence.text: Viral replication occurs during HIV infection.

evidence.text: In developing regions, the prevalence was forecasted to decline from 30.2% to 19.3%.

evidence.text: The change in underweight prevalence in Africa was 12% (95% CI, 8%-16%).

evidence.text: Neglected tropical diseases occur primarily in rural areas of low-income countries and fragile states.

evidence.text: Between 1986 and 1992, 44 cases of adult T cell leukaemia/lymphoma were diagnosed in the United Kingdom.

evidence.text: In Africa, the prevalence of underweight was forecasted to increase from 24.0% to 26.8%.

evidence.text: HTLV-II infection is endemic in some native American and African peoples.

evidence.text: HTLV-I infection is associated with a 1-5% lifetime risk of adult T cell leukaemia/lymphoma.

evidence.text: HTLV-I infection is found among immigrants from the endemic regions in Europe.

evidence.text: In 2010, there were an estimated 1.1 million incident cases of tuberculosis among the 34 million people living with HIV worldwide.

evidence.text: TLX1 and TLX3 transcription factor oncogenes have a key role in the pathogenesis of T cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (T-ALL).
evidence.source: Reverse engineering of TLX oncogenic transcriptional networks identifies RUNX1 as tumor suppressor in T-ALL
